Article.ArticleTitle: Awareness and best practices in using ketogenic therapy to treat serious mental illness: a modified Delphi consensus.

Abstract.AbstractText: Metabolic dysfunction is emerging as an important contributor to the pathophysiology of major depressive disorder, bipolar disorder, and schizophrenia, fueling interest in ketogenic metabolic therapy (KMT) as a potentially beneficial intervention for serious mental illness. KMT has been used successfully for decades in treating epilepsy, but evidence for treating mental illness has yet to mature.
This study aimed to produce expert-informed guidance for the implementation of KMT in adults with serious mood and psychotic disorders.

A modified Delphi methodology was used to examine the opinions of KMT-experienced mental health experts. A steering group of eight such experts convened to develop an online survey comprising 33 statements regarding 1) the definition of KMT in the context of serious mood and psychotic illness; 2) identification of eligible candidates; 3) monitoring and measurement standards; and 4) best practices in employing KMT. This survey was distributed to clinician peers to examine opinions. The threshold for consensus agreement was set a priori at 75%.
Consensus was reached for all 33 statements (100%); therefore, the steering group approved the complete series of recommendations.
This consensus provides expert-informed guidance to support the use of KMT in adults with major depressive disorder, bipolar disorder, and schizophrenia.
Abstract.CopyrightInformation: Copyright © 2026 Ede, Bernstein, Calabrese, Campbell, Laurent, Palmer, Sethi and Zupec-Kania.

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: Georgia Ede MD PLLC, Newburyport, MA, United States.
Medical Director, Ellenhorn LLC and Accord Mental Health LLC, Arlington, MA, United States.
Touchpoints180, South Windsor, CT, United States.
Division of Psychiatry, Center for Clinical Brain Sciences, University of Edinburgh, Kennedy Tower, Royal Edinburgh Hospital, Edinburgh, United Kingdom.
Licensed Mental Health Counselor, Family Renewal Inc, Vancouver, WA, United States.
Harvard Medical School, McLean Hospital, Belmont, MA, United States.
Department of Psychiatry and Behavioral Sciences, Stanford University, Stanford, CA, United States.
Ketogenic Therapies LLC, Elm Grove, WI, United States.

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: MB was the Chief Medical Officer at Ellenhorn LLC which provides comprehensive psychosocial recovery services for those with complex mental health struggles. He was the Chief Executive Officer and Chief Medical Officer at Accord Mental Health LLC, which provides immersive metabolic treatments, including ketogenic diets, for those with physical and mental health disorders. He served on the scientific and clinical advisory boards for the non-profit organizations Metabolic Mind, The Metabolic Revolution, and The Coalition for Metabolic Health and sat on the scientific advisory boards for Meru Health and MetabolicWise. LC was the Founder and Medical Director of Touchpoints180, a physician-led organization providing educational, consultative, and programmatic services supporting metabolic brain and body health, metabolic health consultations and ketogenic services, and free metabolic-forward community events. She served on the Clinician Advisory Committee for Baszucki Group and as a Clinical Advisor to the non-profits Metabolic Mind and Coalition for Metabolic Health. IC received research funding from Baszucki Group, UKRI Medical Research Council and The Wellcome Trust. He received speaker honoraria from the Society of Biological Psychiatry, Metabolic Health Summit, The Nutritional Medicine Institute and the Psychiatry Redefined Conference. He received consulting fees from the Mayo Clinic, Abbott Lingo, and Baszucki Group. He served on lived experience advisory panels for the UKRI MRC Mental Health Platform, Baszucki Group and Metabolic Mind, and the scientific advisory panel for Abbott Lingo, Metabolic Collective and Keto Live. GE served on the scientific and clinical advisory boards for Metabolic Mind, Metabolic Revolution, the Coalition for Metabolic Health, Children's Mental Health Resource Center, Owna Health, Meru Health, Accord, and the Healing Depression Project. She earned tuition fees from her CME training program Ketogenic Diets for Mental Health, received honoraria for speaking about metabolic psychiatry at medical conferences, and received royalties from her book Change Your Diet, Change Your Mind. NL was employed by and owns Family Renewal, Inc. She had a financial relationship with Ellenhorn through an honorarium for serving on the clinical advisory board of the Accord Program. She offered professional trainings through PESI and serves on clinical advisory boards for the nonprofit organizations Metabolic Mind, Metabolic Collective, Keto Live Project e.V., Institute for Personalized Therapeutic Nutrition (IPTN), and the Brain Inflammation Collaborative. CP was the founder and owner of MH2, a medical practice that provides comprehensive mental health care that may include metabolic and ketogenic therapies. He received honoraria for speaking at conferences on ketogenic therapies and received royalties from his book Brain Energy. He served on scientific and advisory boards for the nonprofit organizations Metabolic Mind, Metabolic Revolution, Countdown for a Cure, and the Coalition for Metabolic Health. SS was a cofounder of Metabolic Psychiatry Labs and has served as a scientific advisor for Found Health and Metabolic Mind. BZ-K was a private practice consultant and sole owner/employee of Ketogenic Therapies, LLC and was a paid consultant to the following non-profits: The Charlie Foundation for Ketogenic Therapies. Children's Mental Health Resource Center, and Metabolic Mind. She received an honorarium to speak at the 2025 Food and Nutrition Conference and Expo, and is a volunteer consultant to Accord Mental Health, Glut1 Deficiency Foundation, and Anorexit.
